current.summary: Architectural rethink of Agent Skills spec: moving from metadata convention refinement to foundational design of spec structure

activeWork.description: Agent Skills specification v0.1 — determining architectural approach (sidecar file vs. extend existing spec vs. new spec entirely) before fixing implementation details
activeWork.progress: Identified 5 core pain points from research: activation reliability, no safety signal, no versioning, poor portability, no distribution story. User requested architectural pivot to reconsider spec foundation. Content_hash edge case analysis paused pending architecture decision.
blockers: Architectural decision needed: sidecar file vs. extend existing spec vs. new spec entirely
Map 5 pain points to spec solutions (what does each pain point require?)
Balance community adoptability with realistic implementation scope

recentDecisions: Pivoted from incremental refinement to foundational architectural rethink
Committed research outputs (16 files, 2,205 lines) before architecture review
nextSteps: Map 5 pain points to potential spec solutions: which problems require which features?
Evaluate 3 architectural approaches (sidecar, integrated, new) against pain point map
Assess community adoptability and realistic implementation scope for each approach
Make architecture decision and document rationale
Design spec structure based on chosen architecture

context.notes: Previous session completed deep community sentiment research (15 files) + 6-persona adversarial review of metadata convention. Spec has zero community contributions in 3.5 months. Current session: user recognized we were optimizing details on unvalidated structure. Wants to start from actual problems (5 pain points) and design spec architecture to solve them, rather than incrementally improving current metadata convention. No time pressure — emphasizing thoughtfulness and realism over speed.

previousSessions.summary: Deep research on Agent Skills spec community sentiment, then adversarial review of metadata convention provenance design, leading to v0.1 strategy decisions
completedWork: Deep community sentiment research across GitHub, Reddit, HN, blogs, Twitter — 15 research files
Mapped all existing solution proposals for top 5 pain points
Discovered spec has ZERO community contributions in 3.5 months
6-persona adversarial review of full metadata convention
6-persona deep dive specifically on provenance design
Made two key strategy decisions: v0.1 ships with complete trust story (signed attestation, not unsigned), and outreach starts with agent ecosystem folks
nextSteps: Fix content_hash edge cases identified by panel (CRLF/binary, blanking regex, symlinks, unicode)
Evaluate SSH vs Sigstore for attestation signing — make decision
Build reference implementation for content_hash with 20+ test vectors
Prepare metadata convention for agent ecosystem community review
Reach out to agent ecosystem folks for real human review of provenance section
